source: CT6-6 requirements §1 (DD-1 … DD-18): a self-contextualizing data-dictionary skill — component 1 of the in-repo tier

scenarios: end-to-end build corroborates a false key claim and a non-DB join
a wrong claimed type on a non-key field is flagged
acceptance_criteria: value-level corroboration of every provided def — key + type claims (DD-14)
by-field/by-table reference map + relational/blend map incl. code joins (DD-7)

scenarios: the contract documents the vocabulary, sequencing, and maintenance
acceptance_criteria: recursive code analysis following connection-masking objects (DD-3/4)
sequenced after codebase+integration mapping + freshness check (DD-6/16)
does not re-implement deterministic pieces in prose

scenarios: no-DB path never fabricates live data
an all-null sampled column is inference, not live-data
acceptance_criteria: build_from_inputs no-DB path never marks live-data
live-data only on a non-null sampled value
MIN_KEY_SAMPLE hedge: small-sample inferred key never above medium
modelled on the *-mapping family; complementary to data-lineage-mapping
skill count 42 with data-dictionary entry across CLAUDE.md/CODEBASE_MAP/README
remediation pins: non-key corroboration, empty table, all-null column, small-sample hedge, populated reference-map render, no-DB path, live_inspection block, quoted-identifier crash
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only engine + a skill contract + tests + docs).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — no service API; the engine's live path is dogfooded against a local throwaway SQLite DB (the honest stand-in for DD-9/10), exercised by the pytest suite.
testing_authorizations.both_layer_front_to_back: N/A — single-layer (Python engine + skill doctrine) change.
